body.title: Engram programming docs: Pursuit as first-class architectural concept
body.result: Programming guide companion to pursuit_layer.md. Goal-is-everywhere principle, wrap/skip criteria, minimal template, two worked examples (reply, BOREDOM-nested), common mistakes section, MVP spec for test-case. lab/design_docs/pursuit_programming.md + .dsb companion. Commit ef4fa78d.
body.description:
Write Pursuit into Igor's engram programming docs as a first-class architectural concept. This is the practitioner-facing complement to T-pursuit-layer-design-doc (which is the architectural design reference).

This doc is the 'how to write Pursuits' guide — not just what they are, but when to use them and what the pattern looks like.

Doc must cover:

1. When a behavior needs a Pursuit (not just an engram)
   - Rule of thumb: if the behavior involves committing to a goal across more than one sub-action, it's a Pursuit
   - Heuristic: 'would a human get a small dopamine hit from starting this?' → needs a Pursuit
   - Examples: scanning for work, taking on a ticket, replying when there's context to preserve, checking logs, etc.

2. The Pursuit template pattern
   - How to write the goal-facia formation step
   - How to emit the anticipation event (what shape, what metadata)
   - How to spawn sub-Pursuits
   - How to write the completion test + ding

3. Worked examples (based on T-single-pursuit-test-case output)
   - BOREDOM → scan-for-work Pursuit → foreman_scan → found-work → do-ticket Pursuit
   - Reply Pursuit (T-reply-forms-pursuit)

4. The goal-is-everywhere principle
   - Recalibrate expectations: goals are not just epics. The word 'goal' now means any Pursuit's target.
   - Old mental model: goals = big ambitions
   - New mental model: goals = everywhere, at every layer of commitment

5. Layer table
   | Layer | Unit | Analogy | Igor primitive |
   | Cells | engram cell | assembly instruction | BRANCHIF, FORKIF |
   | Engrams | engram chain | function / high-level code | pe_* tools |
   | Pursuits | Pursuit | process / goal-bound unit | goal_facia + dopamine events |

File location: adjacent to existing engram docs (find and confirm location before writing).
Gates: T-pursuit-layer-design-doc must be complete — programming docs reference the design doc.

Test plan:
- All 5 sections present
- Worked examples runnable / verifiable
- Goal-is-everywhere section clear — no residual 'goals = big things' language
- Reviewed by Akien
